methods.description: Recording air temperature and relative humidity inside and outside caves using data loggers mounted to avoid bedrock thermal influence, with data collected at one-hour resolution.

steps.description: recording meteorological parameters (air temperature and relative humidity) outside and inside the caves
steps.searchable_summary: Record air temperature and relative humidity outside and inside caves

steps.description: Microclimate data were measured at one-hour resolution by HOBO U23 Pro v2 Temperature/Relative Humidity Data Loggers.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure microclimate data using HOBO U23 Pro v2 loggers
inputs: HOBO U23 Pro v2 Data Loggers

steps.description: Loggers were mounted at 1.5-2.0 m above the cave floor and 2-3 cm off the wall to avoid recording the thermal signal of the bedrock
steps.searchable_summary: Mount data loggers at specified height and distance from wall

steps.description: Microclimate data were measured at one-hour resolution
steps.searchable_summary: Record microclimate data at one-hour resolution

methods.description: Collection of monthly-integrated rainwater samples using various systems, including plastic containers with funnels and anti-evaporation measures (paraffin oil or specific systems), for subsequent isotopic analysis. Daily precipitation data from external sources were also obtained.

steps.description: Monthly-integrated rainwater samples have been collected above ZB, MP, SP [35] NG, LOK and MOD caves [25] in plastic containers with funnel protected by PVC net to prevent clogging
steps.searchable_summary: Collect monthly-integrated rainwater samples using plastic containers with funnel and net

steps.description: and a layer of paraffin oil to hamper the evaporation of collected rainwater, as this significantly effects water isotopic composition.
steps.searchable_summary: Add paraffin oil layer to rainwater samples to prevent evaporation

steps.description: During the study of MOD cave by Rudzka et al., [37], a Pluvimate ® rain-gauge was used in order to collect rainwater and record its intensity.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect rainwater and record intensity using a Pluvimate rain-gauge (specific site)

steps.description: At the GB/DB and DC sites composite monthly samples of precipitation were collected by simple anti-evaporation system without an oil film
steps.searchable_summary: Collect composite monthly precipitation samples using simple anti-evaporation system without oil (specific sites)

steps.description: while at VEL, SIR and LUK sites the system of Gröning et al. [56] was employed [39].
steps.searchable_summary: Collect precipitation samples using the Gröning et al. system (specific sites)

steps.description: Daily precipitation amount data were obtained from adjacent meteorological stations operated by the Croatian Meteorological and Hydrological Service (CMHS).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain daily precipitation data from external meteorological stations

steps.description: measuring the isotopic composition (δ 2 H and δ 18 O) of rain ... water [25,35,36]. Analytical methods related to stable isotope measurements used in Rudzka et al., [37], Lončar [40], Surić et al., [25,35], Czuppon et al., [36] and Paar et al. [39] are given in detail in Appendix A.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure stable isotopic composition (δ2H, δ18O) of rainwater samples

methods.method_name: Cave Drip Water Sampling for Isotopic and Elemental Analysis
methods.description: Collection of cave drip water samples from various sites (including those feeding stalagmites) and with different frequencies (monthly-integrated, daily, single samples with variable accumulation) for subsequent isotopic and elemental analysis. Samples were collected without anti-evaporation oil due to high cave humidity.

steps.description: Monthly-integrated drip water samples were collected under the drip sites that had been feeding stalagmites predetermined for paleoclimate studies in NG, LOK, MOD, ZB, MP, SP, DB, GB, DC, as well as from several other drip points.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect monthly-integrated drip water samples from specific drip sites

steps.description: In-cave water containers did not include protective paraffin oil since the cave air RH was 100%, as anticipated, so no evaporation was expected.
steps.searchable_summary: Do not add paraffin oil to in-cave drip water samples (due to high RH)

steps.description: Thus, along the VEL profile, 1 to 6 samples were collected daily during five days of caving, while in LUK and SIR only one sample of water in each cave was collected [39].
steps.searchable_summary: Collect drip water samples daily during caving trips (specific sites) or single samples (other specific sites)
outputs: collected drip water samples (daily or single)

steps.description: In island caves (SP, MED, VRD, KRA, MML, VML) the single samples were also collected per drip site, with drip water accumulating from 2 to 102 days [40,41].
steps.searchable_summary: Collect single drip water samples per drip site with variable accumulation periods (island caves)
outputs: collected drip water samples (single, variable accumulation)

steps.description: All drip water samples were taken from the exact sites from where speleothems have been collected for paleoenvironmental reconstruction.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure drip water samples are collected from sites corresponding to speleothem collection locations

steps.description: measuring the isotopic composition (δ 2 H and δ 18 O) of ... cave drip water [25,35,36]. Analytical methods related to stable isotope measurements used in Rudzka et al., [37], Lončar [40], Surić et al., [25,35], Czuppon et al., [36] and Paar et al. [39] are given in detail in Appendix A.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure stable isotopic composition (δ2H, δ18O) of drip water samples
outputs: drip water isotopic composition data (δ2H, δ18O)

steps.description: Additionally, analyses of drip water elemental composition were performed at DB and GB sites [36] and in MOD cave (2012-2013, unpublished)
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze elemental composition of drip water samples (specific sites)

methods.description: Collection of newly deposited calcite on various artificial substrates (glass plates, plastic funnels, nylon sheet) placed under active drip sites. Some sites used light bulbs to imitate stalagmite surfaces. This method ensures a known deposition period, avoiding the youngest parts of natural stalagmites. Collected calcite is used for stable isotope measurements.

steps.description: Modern calcite was collected on pre-cleaned glass plates (10 × 10 cm) in MP caves
steps.searchable_summary: Collect modern calcite on glass plates (specific site)
outputs: collected modern calcite on glass plates

steps.description: on plastic funnels and glass plates in SP cave [35]
steps.searchable_summary: Collect modern calcite on plastic funnels and glass plates (specific site)
outputs: collected modern calcite on funnels and plates

steps.description: and on a nylon sheet in MOD cave [43].
steps.searchable_summary: Collect modern calcite on a nylon sheet (specific site)
outputs: collected modern calcite on nylon sheet

steps.description: Farming of modern calcite in DB and GB caves was performed by placing light bulbs under active drip sites, imitating the rounded stalagmite surface [36].
steps.searchable_summary: Farm modern calcite by placing light bulbs under drip sites to imitate stalagmite surface (specific sites)
outputs: setup for calcite farming with light bulbs

steps.description: We avoided sampling of the youngest parts of the active stalagmites since with the artificial material we were sure about the period of deposition.
steps.searchable_summary: Avoid sampling youngest parts of active stalagmites, prefer artificial substrates for known deposition period

steps.description: Analytical methods related to stable isotope measurements used in Rudzka et al., [37], Lončar [40], Surić et al., [25,35], Czuppon et al., [36] and Paar et al. [39] are given in detail in Appendix A.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure stable isotopic composition of modern calcite samples

methods.description: Recording the discharge dynamics (drip rate) of cave drips using automated acoustic Stalagmate counters to understand aquifer architecture.

steps.description: NG, LOK, MOD, ZB, MP and SP caves were also equipped with automated acoustic Stalagmate ® drip counters [59] to record discharge dynamics.
steps.searchable_summary: Equip caves with automated acoustic Stalagmate drip counters

steps.description: to record discharge dynamics.
steps.searchable_summary: Record drip discharge dynamics using automated counters
